status: partial_result_likely_valid (Problem 1(ii): JF^*(Q(Omega)) is nonseparable for Euclidean-ball and ell_infty-ball families; Problem 1(i), ell_1 noncontainment, remains open)
search_text: 1210.2379 Function spaces not containing ell_1 Argyros Manoussakis Petrakis Problem 1 JF(Q(Omega)) Euclidean balls ell_infty balls convex bodies nonseparable dual. Partial theorem: if Q contains an uncountable subfamily of positive-measure measurable sets pairwise distinct modulo null sets, then the integration functionals V^*(f)=int_V f form an uncountable 1-separated subset of JF(Q)^*. Normalized indicators chi_A/mu(A) have JF(Q)-norm at most 1 by disjointness, so distinct mod-null sets are separated by U\V or V\U. Applies to Euclidean balls and ell_infty^d balls contained in any nonempty open Omega by varying a small radius. This answers part (ii) for the ball-family cases singled out as open; it does not prove part (i) that JF(Q(Omega)) does not contain ell_1.
